name: Li Yu
bio: Li Yu, a celebrated contemporary Chinese western frontier poet, was born in Chongqing with ancestral roots in Chao County, Anhui. He grew up in Wuhan and wandered across much of China, but spent most of his life amid the snows and lush shade at the northern foot of the Tianshan Mountains. The eternal themes of love and death have bound themselves inseparably to his work. His poetry and creative career have been broadcast internationally by China Radio International in over a dozen languages, and translated into multiple ethnic minority languages within China. His published works include the poetry collections *Verses from Junggar*, *Ah, the Waters of the Ili River Bleached My Army Uniform*, *War and the City*, *For Love, Baghdad Is Not Too Far*, and the prose collection *Alley Series*.
